data.purpose: Defines coding session workflow. General — not project-bound. Load explicitly at the start of any implementation session.

context.user_background: C# and Java. Currently transitioning to Python.
context.condition: This background is the condition under which the session structure below applies. Reassess if language or familiarity level changes significantly.

session_structure.condition: Applies until user is trained enough to begin modifying or contributing code, at which point workflow is explicitly reassessed.

session_structure.rule: Walk through code together line by line
session_structure.condition: Applies in early familiarity phase. Either party flags anything unclear, wrong, or assumption-laden. Stop and resolve before moving on.

session_structure.rule: As familiarity grows, shift to targeted review of non-obvious parts
session_structure.condition: Transition point is not fixed — flagged when line-by-line is producing diminishing returns.

session_structure.rule: Code presentation format: chunk then explanation, interleaved. Never dump the full file followed by a wall of explanation.
session_structure.condition: Applies always. One logical unit at a time — code chunk first, explanation immediately after, then next chunk. Logical unit sizing is Claude's judgment: imports, result types, a single function group. Never larger than what fits on one screen without scrolling.

conceptual_mistakes.definition: Two types: (1) misalignment between what user intends and what Claude produces, (2) blindspots in Claude's own code — assumptions not surfaced, patterns carried over incorrectly from other languages.
conceptual_mistakes.handling: Both caught during walkthrough. Claude names its own mistakes directly when found. No softening.

mobile_briefing.rule: BRIEFING.md in the perspective repo is the mobile session briefing file. Update it at the end of every PC session where progress was made.
mobile_briefing.update_trigger: Any of: phase status change, new architectural decision, new open question, environment change.
mobile_briefing.update_content: Current phase and status, what exists, next task, any decisions made this session that affect mobile work.
mobile_briefing.condition: Applies to all PC sessions on project perspective. Stale briefing file = wasted mobile session time.
mobile_briefing.mobile_session_prompt: Paste BRIEFING.md content and add: 'No GitHub access. Mobile session. [your question or task]'

projects.description: Project Perspective — Representation Space Traversal AI
projects.note: DGL rejected due to broken Windows packaging for PyTorch 2.5. PyTorch Geometric selected as graph compute backend.

notes: Migrated from workflow.txt session 9. Updated session 10, 2026-05-01: added code presentation format rule. Updated session 10, 2026-05-02: removed duplicate rule, added mobile briefing maintenance rule, updated current phase to P2.
